  "findings": [
    "Brake hose is twisted, causing mechanical stress and potential restriction of brake fluid flow.",
    "Risk of premature hose failure, chafing against suspension components, or sudden loss of braking pressure."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Remove the brake caliper or disconnect the hose to untwist and properly align it.",
    "Inspect the hose for internal or external damage, cracking, or chafing, and replace if damaged.",
    "Bleed the brake system and verify proper clearance through the full range of steering and suspension travel."
  ],
  "rationale": "A twisted brake hose is a severe installation defect that subjects the hose to constant torsional stress, increases the risk of fatigue failure, and can cause the hose to rub against moving suspension or steering components. This violates standard FMVSS-adjacent safety practices and OEM installation specifications, requiring immediate corrective action.",
  "references": [
    "FMVSS 106 (Brake Hoses)",
    "OEM Brake Service Manual Guidelines"
  ]
}
